item.title: In the heart of the wilderness, something extraordinary unfolded.
item.description: Source https://hasanjasim.online/bees-swarm-rhino-peaceful-rest-on-giant-beast-natures-sanctuary/ A swarm of bees on a mission to build a new home paused to rest. But instead of perching on a branch, wall, or tree, they chose a living, breathing island: A Rhinoceros. Swarming bees aren’t aggressive. They’re tired, vulnerable, but fiercely protective of their queen if they perceive a threat (so make sure you aren't one). As they search for the perfect spot to start a new colony, they often stop to regroup. Yet it’s almost unheard of to see them settle on the thick, armored back of one of Earth’s largest land mammals. The Rhino is like "yeah what ever".
item.dc:creator: Rastifaan

item.title: Castellum Divisorium engineers were ahead of their time.
item.description: The Castellum Divisorium was a Roman hydraulic engineering structure—essentially a central water distribution tank or basin. Once water reached the city (via aqueducts), the castellum divisorium would divide the flow into multiple channels, usually leading to public fountains, baths, and private users. Role of Engineers Roman engineers in charge of the castellum divisorium had to: Design hydraulic distribution: ensuring proportional flow to different outlets by using calibrated bronze orifices (calices) that regulated water volume. Maintain pressure balance: controlling the head of water so that distant or elevated districts still received supply. Inspect flow integrity: preventing unauthorized tapping or widening of the outlet pipes, which was a common form of water theft. Oversee construction: building durable cisterns of concrete faced with opus caementicium, often lined with waterproof pozzolana mortar. Document allocations: engineers worked with the curatores aquarum (officials of the aqueducts) to record official water rights and monitor delivery. Notable Figures Frontinus (1st c. CE), author of De Aquaeductu Urbis Romae, describes castella divisorium in detail and the duties of water engineers in Rome. Local municipal engineers (sometimes attached to the collegia fabrum) were tasked with upkeep in provincial towns. Engineering Principles Gravity-fed system: No pumps—flow depended entirely on altitude differentials. Three-tiered division: typically, one channel for public fountains (first priority), one for baths, and one for private concessions. Modular orifices: each bronze outlet had a fixed diameter, corresponding to a legal unit of water (the quinaria), ensuring fairness and traceability.
item.dc:creator: ArcadesCinza

item.title: BOROWITZ: Please give us whatever deal you gave Ghislaine
item.description: KYIV (The Borowitz Report)—Seeking robust security guarantees for his nation, on Wednesday Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy asked Donald J. Trump to “give Ukraine the same protection you have given pedophiles.” Hoping to flatter Trump, Zelenskyy said, “Mr. President, the Ukrainian people ask you to stand strong with them, just as you have stood strong in your refusal to release the Epstein files.” “We are not, of course, a nation of pedophiles,” he continued. “But imagine that we were, and protect us accordingly.” Specifying the sort of protection he sought for Ukraine, Zelenskyy ended his charm offensive by beseeching Trump, “Please give us whatever deal you gave Ghislaine.”
item.dc:creator: JasonM1

item.description: In 1969, a secretary accidentally invented the correction fluid that would save millions of students from rewriting entire papers. But Bette Nesmith Graham's journey from single mom to millionaire inventor is one of the most inspiring underdog stories in business history... Bette Nesmith Graham was a single mother working as a secretary in Dallas in the 1950s when electric typewriters made her job much harder. Unlike older typewriters, mistakes on electric models couldn't be easily erased, forcing secretaries to retype entire pages for single errors. Graham watched artists painting a bank's holiday windows and had a brilliant idea. In her kitchen, she mixed white tempera paint with a common solvent to create a correction fluid. She secretly used it at work for months, painting over her typing mistakes and letting them dry before typing the correction on top. When other secretaries noticed her error-free work, they begged for the formula. Graham began mixing batches in her kitchen blender and selling bottles from her home. She called it 'Mistake Out,' later renamed 'Liquid Paper.' Despite being fired from her secretarial job for spending too much time on her side business, she persisted. By 1968, she was producing a million bottles annually from a converted garage. In 1979, Graham sold Liquid Paper to Gillette Corporation for $47.5 million plus royalties. She used her wealth to establish foundations supporting women in business and the arts. Interestingly, her son Mike Nesmith became famous as one of The Monkees, but even he admitted his mother's invention had more lasting impact than his music career.
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: Ms. Rachel, ever the beacon of humanity, a shining example, and a ray of hope.
item.description: Another hot take: Groups like the ADL and “StopAntisemitism” are crying wolf. By slapping the label of antisemitism on pro-Israel politics that have nothing to do with hatred of Jews, they’ve drained the word of its power. When everything is called antisemitism, nothing is. And that means when real antisemitism appears, people won’t believe it, leaving Jewish communities more vulnerable than ever.
item.dc:creator: cheekysheikhy

item.title: On Sept 3rd, some of Trump's victims will testify to Congress about his involvement with Epstein - I pray these brave women and their families are protected.
item.description: The tip of the iceberg of Trump's pedophilia and sexual assaults: https://www.courtlistener.com/docket/4154484/1/katie-johnson-v-donald-j-trump/ https://storage.courtlistener.com/recap/gov.uscourts.cacd.646485/gov.uscourts.cacd.646485.1.0_1.pdf It is a remarkable coincidence how key persons who are in a position to divulge crucial information about Trump's sexual abuse and raping of minors have committed suicide before they can provide evidence: — Epstein "hanging himself" while in prison just before his trial that would have introduced damning evidential proof about Trump's rapes - while Epstein was in federal prison managed by the first Trump administration. Like Nixon's Watergate tapes, critical prison surveillance recordings mysteriously vanished, allegedly due to "technical issues" at the time of his "suicide". — Virginia Giuffre, who allegedly committed suicide four months ago, just as the Epstein Files were becoming a politically damaging news story. — Carolyn Adriano, who was 14 years old when she was raped, provided crucial testimony in the conviction of Ghislaine Maxwell in 2021. She allegedly committed suicide two years later in Palm Beach County near Mar-a-Lago, before being questioned about other participants in Epstein’s circle who might have raped her, including Trump. Ghislaine Maxwell, who had two days of private meetings with Trump’s defense attorney, Todd Blanche, was immediately transferred - ignoring protocol and rules - to the most minimal security prison in the country: a prison that is not permitted to accept sex offenders and traffickers. The reason might be twofold: the “country club prison” is like a school campus and provides Maxwell with a cushy lifestyle in return for her false testimony exonerating Trump. In addition, the facility lacks surveillance cameras and would be the perfect setting for Maxwell to “commit suicide” if the Trump organization feels that she remains a liability. Trump has the power — not the authority, but the power — to make any death appear to be an "accident" or a "suicide". After corrupting his pardoning power to free more than a thousand insurrectionists from prison and giving them federal policing power, Trump can have these convicted violent felons demonstrate their ability to make someone disappear, or make good on his threat to have a victim’s loved ones experience tragedy. These “agents” can even convince someone to end their own life in order to protect their family. Trump is exploiting the absolute immunity and authoritarian power that our fascist SCOTUS and Congress have bestowed on him. --------
item.dc:creator: JackArmstrong2000

22

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: This pea, spinach and green onion risotto, with chicken is brought to you by @DocHarriers You can see other goodies in his posts and the recipe for this one is here: https://imgur.com/gallery/pea-spinach-green-onion-risotto-with-chicken-DcY6fcr
27 Man Tax: Misha Collins
“Listen to me, mister. You’re my knight in shining armor. Don’t you forget it.” - Ethel Thayer I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting on it, maybe let it go, don't comment on it and find something that you do like. Seriously y'all. Coming to only comment "omg that's AI" bums me out. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server so you guys can donate memes to the museum for future CCC posts. I don't want to run out! Also, we can hang out, play some trivia, talk about movies & tv and, apparently, sleepyitis, Monty Python and the Holy Grail and RACK-oons. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator
Icantanymore242

item.title: Christopher Chaplin,born in 1962, is the youngest son of iconic comedian Charlie Chaplin and his fourth wife, Oona O’Neill. Charlie, aged 73 at Christopher’s birth, had already cemented his legacy as a silent film legend
item.description: Christopher Chaplin, born in 1962, is the youngest son of iconic comedian Charlie Chaplin and his fourth wife, Oona O’Neill. Charlie, aged 73 at Christopher’s birth, had already cemented his legacy as a silent film legend, creating timeless works like The Kid and Modern Times. Christopher grew up in Switzerland, where the family lived after Charlie’s exile from the U.S. due to political controversies. Raised in a large family with seven siblings, Christopher pursued a career in acting and music, distinct from his father’s towering fame. Charlie’s later years were marked by a quieter life, focusing on family and creative projects, with Christopher born during this reflective period. Despite the age gap, their bond reflected Charlie’s enduring charisma and devotion.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Zodiacs as Gods - Makeup - MCroft07
item.description:
Aries
Sagittarius
Taurus
Virgo
Capricorn
Leo
Gemini
Cancer
Aquarius
Pisces
Libra
Scorpio I designed all of these based on reading about zodiacs (I know nothing) and talking to people about what they feel like represents them the most who do like looking at their star signs. They're original designs and I built the headpieces too. Sharing since most of you guys didn't know that I also do a lot of these and asked me to share more, I don't just do character paints, those are just the Saturday paints that I do. However, I also paint Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday with other designs that are based on my face and chest :) These are actually old now, I did them in 2021, but a lot of people still like them :) I'm a Capricorn (December 31)
item.dc:creator: MCroft07
